meta.problem: A customer-facing Hirly feature: given a candidate-evidence profile and one job post, produce a tailored, evidence-traceable application draft — never inventing experience, always blocking unsupported claims and asking the candidate for what only they can answer.

agent.name: evidence-backed-application-agent
agent.model_rationale: Newest Opus-class — the default; the job is evidence-mapping and claim-verification, where rigor matters more than speed/cost.
agent.system_summary: Given a candidate-evidence file and a job post (URL, with pasted text as fallback), extracts material requirements and application questions, maps each requirement to exact candidate evidence, drafts a tailored application, blocks unsupported/uncertain/conflicting claims, asks the candidate for anything only they can answer, and writes application-pack.md, claim-map.json, and submission-plan.json to /mnt/session/outputs/.
never_dos: Never present an unsupported, uncertain, or conflicting claim as fact
Never fill salary expectation, work authorization, relocation, legal attestations, or demographic/personal answers unless explicitly provided
Never claim an application was submitted or that any callback/interview/offer/outcome occurred
Never fabricate or infer job requirements if the job page cannot be read reliably — stop and ask for pasted job text instead
Never write generic, non-job-specific application content

outcome.description: Produce application-pack.md, claim-map.json, and submission-plan.json for the supplied candidate + job, fully evidence-traceable and never overstating readiness to submit.
rubric_criteria: Every material application claim has at least one valid candidate-evidence reference (evidence_id)
No unsupported, uncertain, or conflicting claim is presented as fact
Salary expectations, work authorization, relocation, legal attestations, demographic information, and other personal answers are left for the candidate unless explicitly provided
The draft is specific to the supplied job, not generic
Job requirements and draft claims remain traceable to their sources
The output never claims an application was submitted or that a callback, interview, offer, or job outcome occurred

connections.notes: v0 is analysis + drafting only — no delivery connector, no submission. Web fetch reads the public job posting; pasted job text is the fallback when the page can't be read reliably.

schedule.human: Triggered manually today; later by Hirly's host application per candidate/job pair — not a recurring clock job.

cases.name: case-01-lssm-tech-builder
cases.expected: Strong-fit case: LSSM Architect Network posting vs. a senior product/full-stack engineer — used for the live v0 launch run. Tests uncertain-claim blocking on Kubernetes/team-size/SOC2 gaps.

cases.name: case-02-grace-head-of-product-design
cases.expected: Strong-fit, held back: GRACE Head of Product & Design vs. a bilingual Paris-based product/design lead. Tests the insurtech-audit uncertain claim and B2B2C-vs-enterprise-B2B distinction.

cases.name: case-03-brex-enterprise-ae
cases.expected: Strong-fit, held back: Brex Enterprise AE vs. a quota-carrying B2B SaaS AE with direct financial-software vertical fit. Tests the accounting-platform-exposure uncertain claim and the US-work-authorization candidate-only question.

evals.notes: Each case pairs a matched synthetic candidate with its job posting so fit is realistic; each candidate still carries deliberate unsupported/uncertain/candidate-only gaps to exercise the blocking behavior. Case 1 is the v0 build/launch input; cases 2-3 are held back as a regression check before promoting any new agent version.

defaults_applied.write_actions: None — v0 drafts only, no real submission, no production credentials, no real candidate PII

interface.who: Théo (today); Hirly's host application and its candidates later
interface.how: Deliverables land as files (application-pack.md, claim-map.json, submission-plan.json); reopened via the overview page and Console for now.

next_directions.what: Sandbox or single-ATS submission tool controlled by the Hirly host application
next_directions.why_deferred: Write-action into an external system — requires explicit candidate approval of the exact current submission plan and independent confirmation before it's safe
next_directions.how: A custom tool (or MCP connector for the target ATS) gated always_ask, that: (1) requires the candidate to approve the exact submission-plan.json content immediately before the call, (2) executes at most one submission, (3) independently observes confirmation (not a model-asserted success), (4) returns a submission receipt. The model must never generate its own approval or treat a button click alone as proof of successful submission.

next_directions.what: Wire ContractSpec / CompanyOS as the application-side contract and enforcement boundary
next_directions.why_deferred: Out of scope for this workshop build by explicit choice — today's behavior is implemented directly with CMA primitives
next_directions.how: Once v1's submission tool exists, front it with a ContractSpec contract defining the submission precondition (candidate approval + evidence completeness) and CompanyOS as the enforcement/observability layer

next_directions.what: Multiagent orchestration, memory store, and scheduled deployment
next_directions.why_deferred: Explicitly out of scope for this workshop iteration — one agent, one candidate, one job per run, on-demand only
next_directions.how: Not planned as a near-term next step; revisit only if Hirly's host application needs cross-run memory (e.g. a per-candidate evidence store) or batch/recurring triggering

next_directions.what: Lock environment networking to job-board domains only
next_directions.why_deferred: Hardening — unrestricted is acceptable while the agent is read-only and no credentials are in play
next_directions.how: Switch environment.networking to limited with the relevant job-board hosts in allowed_hosts once the target ATS/job sources are known

next_directions.what: Re-run the 3 eval cases before promoting any new agent version
next_directions.why_deferred: Process habit, not a build task
next_directions.how: Run evals/run-evals.sh against the new version; only update behavior when verdicts hold across best/partial/poor-fit cases
